title: onobrychis viciifolia subsp. arenaria
intro: Onobrychis viciifolia subsp. arenaria, commonly known as ashy sainfoin or sainfoin, is a plant species within the pea family that is native to the deserts and dry regions of Europe and Asia. The medicinal properties of onobrychis viciifolia subsp. arenaria include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been utilized in traditional medicine for centuries to treat various health issues. The health benefits of consuming this plant include reducing symptoms of menopause, alleviating menstrual cramps, and lowering blood sugar levels. Onobrychis viciifolia subsp. arenaria can be consumed as an herbal tea, made by infusing the dried flowers, leaves, or roots in hot water, or as a tincture, which is a concentrated liquid extract of the plant's medicinal properties.
